en.extract:
The Cameroon olive pigeon (Columba sjostedti) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found in the Cameroon line (including Bioko), and is found in pasture habitats with minimal vegetation cover as well as in montane forests. Its diet includes fruit from Polyscias fulva and waterberry trees.

The '''Cameroon olive pigeon''' ('''''Columba sjostedti''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found in the [[Cameroon line]] (including [[Bioko]]<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Cooper |first=Jacob C. |last2=Johnson |first2=Oscar |last3=Davis |first3=Tristan J. |last4=Terrill |first4=Ryan S. |last5=Wolfe |first5=Jared D. |last6=Brzeski |first6=Kristin E. |last7=Etingüe |first7=Amancio M. |last8=Powell |first8=Luke L. |date=February 2017 |title=Notes on the distribution of the avifauna of Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea, including one new country record |url=https://biodiversityinitiative.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/Cooper-et-al.-2017-Notes-on-the-distribution-of-the-avifauna-of-Bioko-Island-Equatorial-Guinea-including-one-new-country-record.pdf |journal=Malimbus |page=11 |via=ResearchGate}}</ref>), and is found in pasture habitats with minimal vegetation cover<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Kamga |first1=Solange Mekuate |last2=Tamungang |first2=Simon Awafor |last3=Awa |first3=Taku |last4=Ewome |first4=Francis Luma |last5=Motombi |first5=Francis Njie |last6=Hořák |first6=David |last7=Riegert |first7=Jan |date=2022-03-19 |title=The Importance of Forest Elephants for Vegetation Structure Modification and Its Influence on the Bird Community of a Mid-Elevation Forest on Mount Cameroon, West-Central Africa |journal=Diversity |volume=14 |issue=3 |page=227 |doi=10.3390/d14030227 |issn=1424-2818|doi-access=free }}</ref> as well as in montane forests.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Bergl |first1=Richard A. |last2=Oates |first2=John F. |last3=Fotso |first3=Roger |date=January 2007 |title=Distribution and protected area coverage of endemic taxa in West Africa's Biafran forests and highlands |journal=Biological Conservation |volume=134 |issue=2 |pages=195–208 |doi=10.1016/j.biocon.2006.08.013 |bibcode=2007BCons.134..195B |issn=0006-3207}}</ref> Its diet includes fruit from ''[[Polyscias fulva]]'' and [[Syzygium guineense|waterberry trees]].<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Borokini |first1=T.I. |last2=Babalola |first2=F.D. |last3=Amusa |first3=T.O. |last4=Ivande |first4=S.T. |last5=Wala |first5=Z.J. |last6=Jegede |first6=O.O. |last7=Tanko |first7=D. |last8=Ihuma |first8=J.O. |date=2012-05-28 |title=Community-based Forest Resources Management in Nigeria: Case study of Ngel Nyaki Forest Reserve, Mambilla Plateau, Taraba State, Nigeria |journal=Journal of Tropical Forestry and Environment |volume=2 |issue=1 |doi=10.31357/jtfe.v2i1.571 |issn=2235-9362|doi-access=free }}</ref>
